summary: Removed the remaining sysdolphin/baselib dobj assembly by matching DObjInfoInit in C and moving the leftover data strings into dobj.c. The PR deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.s, removed its object from obj_files.mk, made DObjInfoInit static in dobj.c, and used string-literal placement tricks to preserve required .data strings that were previously supplied by the assembly file.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.s, which only contained .data strings such as "mobj has unexpected blending flags (0x%x).", "hsdIsDescendantOf(info, &hsdDObj)", "sysdolphin_base_library", and "hsd_dobj".
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk, leaving dobj.c.o as the source for the DObj object code/data.
Converted DObjInfoInit from inline assembly / NON_MATCHING fallback into a C implementation that calls hsdInitClassInfo with string literals and assigns release, amnesia, disp, and load callbacks.
Changed DObjInfoInit to a static function declared locally in dobj.c and removed its public prototype from src/sysdolphin/baselib/dobj.h.
Replaced extern references to assembly-provided string labels with direct string literals in dobj.c, including the OSReport message and hsdInitClassInfo library/class-name strings.

Added HSD_DObjSetDefaultClass in dobj.c with an hsdIsDescendantOf assertion, apparently to force retention/allocation of the assert string even though the PR notes the relevant function is removed in practice.
Added forceStringAllocation, explicitly commented as existing only to make the compiler place otherwise-unused strings into data.
smart_moves: Eliminated a standalone assembly data file by reproducing its required string data through C, allowing dobj.s to be removed from the build entirely.
Used intentionally unreachable or practically-unused C functions to force compiler allocation of strings that still needed to exist in the binary/data layout.
Localized DObjInfoInit as static after moving its implementation into dobj.c, avoiding an unnecessary exported header declaration.
Matched DObjInfoInit by using the class-info macros and direct callback assignments instead of preserving the hand-written assembly stub.

Kept evidence comments in the diff explaining why fake line numbers and forceStringAllocation exist, which helps future maintainers understand that these constructs are matching/data-layout tactics rather than gameplay logic.
decomp_lessons: When an assembly file exists only to provide string data, it may be removable by forcing the same literals to be emitted from C in the correct translation unit.
String-literal placement can be affected by whether the strings are referenced from compiled functions; decomp work may sometimes need helper functions solely to cause data emission.
A function can match once external string labels are replaced with direct literals, provided the resulting data layout still matches.
If a class initializer is only used as an init callback inside the same translation unit, it should be made static and removed from the public header.
Generated or forced assertion strings should be documented clearly when line numbers or call sites are artificial for matching purposes.
naming_conventions: DObjInfoInit is treated as a file-local static initializer for the global HSD_DObjInfo hsdDObj.
The PR retains existing baselib/HSD naming patterns such as HSD_DObjSetDefaultClass, HSD_DObjDisp, HSD_DOBJ_INFO, HSD_CLASS_INFO, and HSD_PARENT_INFO.
Temporary linker-style labels such as lbl_80405494, lbl_80405548, and lbl_80405560 were removed in favor of meaningful string literals where possible.
The helper name forceStringAllocation is explicit about its non-runtime-purpose role, though it is not presented as a general project convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a .s file that contained only .data asciz strings and .balign directives by making C emit equivalent strings.
Used direct string literals in OSReport, HSD_Panic, __assert, and hsdInitClassInfo to replace extern char labels from assembly.
Added forceStringAllocation with assertions for "can not find specified pobj in link.\n", "can not find specified pobj in link.", and "dobj->mobj == mobj" solely to force those strings into data.
Added HSD_DObjSetDefaultClass with an hsdIsDescendantOf assertion to retain the "hsdIsDescendantOf(info, &hsdDObj)" string despite the comment that the function is removed in practice.

Matched DObjInfoInit in C by calling hsdInitClassInfo(HSD_CLASS_INFO(&hsdDObj), HSD_CLASS_INFO(&hsdClass), "sysdolphin_base_library", "hsd_dobj", sizeof(HSD_DObjInfo), sizeof(HSD_DObj)) and then writing callback function pointers.
searchable_terms: can not find specified pobj in link
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove forceStringAllocation-style helper functions after better string/data placement solutions.
Search for other baselib .s files that only contain .data strings and could be removed with the same tactic.
Check whether HSD_DObjSetDefaultClass is referenced or retained only for string allocation in subsequent cleanup work.
Compare other class initializer matches that use HSD_CLASS_INFO, HSD_PARENT_INFO, and hsdInitClassInfo callback assignment patterns.
